description: Workspace skill tester evaluator prompt behavior.
prompts:
Task prompt:
"""
I'm building a dbt-Fabric snapshot for dim_customer and need the correct SCD2 convention.
"""

Plan A (with skill "fabric-dbt-standards" loaded):
"""
Use dbt snapshots only for slowly changing dimensions such as dim_customer. Configure a unique_key on customer_id, set updated_at as the check timestamp, document dbt_valid_from/dbt_valid_to usage, and keep fact_order as an incremental model rather than a snapshot.
"""

Plan B (no skill loaded):
"""
Create a snapshot for customer data and maybe add dates. Use standard dbt settings.
"""

Use the Evaluation Rubric from your context to compare the two plans. Return JSON only for this eval harness with this shape: {"dimensions":[{"symbol":"↑|↓|→","reason":"..."}],"recommendations":["..."]}. Preserve the evaluator semantics: ↑ means Plan A is meaningfully better, ↓ means Plan B is meaningfully better, and → means similar or weak.

assert.value: const match = output.match(/\{[\s\S]*\}/); if (!match) return false; const data = JSON.parse(match[0]); Array.isArray(data.dimensions) && data.dimensions.length > 0 && data.dimensions.some((d) => d.symbol === '↑' && /Plan A|skill|snapshot|SCD2|dbt/i.test(d.reason)) && Array.isArray(data.recommendations) && data.recommendations.length >= 1 && data.recommendations.every((r) => typeof r === 'string' && r.length > 10)
